title: Sobralia ciliata
intro: Sobralia ciliata, also known as the "Ciliated Sobralia," is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Sobralia ciliata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sobralia ciliata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and as an adjunct therapy for fever and inflammation.
taxonomy: Sobralia ciliata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Sobralia ciliata is further divided into the genus Sobralia, which is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Sobralieae. This orchid species is characterized by its unique inflorescence and ciliate labellum. The classification of Sobralia ciliata is also closely related to other Sobralia species within the same family.
